text: Conversational aim elicitation: turn a human's natural-language goal into a proposed, editable aim sub-tree they approve
description: The core "humans express needs → graph" bridge of the cooperation engine. A human states a high-level goal in natural language (text or voice — VoiceView.vue already exists as infra); the system proposes a structured sub-tree of aims (decomposition with value/cost guesses) that the human can edit, reorder, and approve before the agents act. The will stays inspectable and revisable; humans stay final authority. This is what lets non-developers drive the system toward real-world goals, not just engineers editing the graph by hand. Concrete next step: define the elicitation→proposed-subtree→human-approval flow on top of the existing graph + MCP create_aim primitives.

status.comment: Verified 2026-06-27. Base exists: voice-bridge (packages/voice-bridge/src/index.ts) runs a conversational agent with list_aims/get_prioritized_aims/create_aim tools that can create aims from speech. Not built: the specific propose→editable aim sub-tree→human-approves-before-commit flow. Still relevant; voice-bridge is the natural starting point.
